enriched_metadata.objective: Classify breast tumors as malignant or benign based on features computed from digitized images of fine needle aspirate (FNA) of breast masses
enriched_metadata.sources: University of Wisconsin Hospitals, Madison - Original diagnostic data from FNA imaging
enriched_metadata.features_description: 30 real-valued features computed from digitized FNA images: 10 cell nuclei characteristics (radius, texture, perimeter, area, smoothness, compactness, concavity, concave points, symmetry, fractal dimension) with mean, standard error, and worst values for each
enriched_metadata.representativity_description: Digitized Fine Needle Aspirate (FNA) images from breast masses; 569 samples with 357 benign and 212 malignant cases from University of Wisconsin clinical data
enriched_metadata.sample_balance_description: Moderately imbalanced dataset with 62.7% benign cases (357) and 37.3% malignant cases (212), reflecting realistic clinical distribution
enriched_metadata.missing_values_description: No missing values - complete feature measurements for all FNA image samples
enriched_metadata.data_errors_description: High-quality medical dataset with no missing values and well-documented feature measurements from standardized medical imaging procedures
